_meta.purpose: Illustrative Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) tongue-sign associations used to ground Stage-2 report generation.
_meta.disclaimer: Traditional associations for wellness/education only. NOT medical diagnosis. TCM patterns are not validated by modern clinical evidence.
_meta.trigger_grammar: Each card lists `match` conditions over Stage-1 characteristics: {char: [accepted values]}. A card fires when all its conditions are satisfied; specificity = number of matched conditions.

tooth_mk.light: mild tooth marks on the edges
tooth_mk.severe: pronounced tooth marks on the edges

patterns.name: Dampness / Phlegm accumulation
patterns.explanation: A thick, greasy coating is traditionally associated with 'dampness' or phlegm accumulation, often linked in TCM to sluggish digestion or fluid retention.

patterns.name: Heat pattern
patterns.explanation: A yellow coating is traditionally read as a sign of 'heat', sometimes associated with inflammation, stress, or rich diet.

patterns.name: Cold pattern
patterns.explanation: A white coating with a pale body is traditionally associated with a 'cold' or deficient state.

patterns.name: Spleen Qi deficiency with dampness
patterns.explanation: Tooth marks together with a greasy coating are classically associated with 'Spleen Qi deficiency with dampness' — a common TCM pattern linked to digestion and fluid balance.

patterns.name: Qi/Blood deficiency
patterns.explanation: A pale body with tooth marks is traditionally associated with 'Qi/Blood deficiency', often related to energy and circulation.

patterns.name: Yin deficiency with heat
patterns.explanation: Cracks with a darker/red body are traditionally read as 'Yin deficiency' with heat — associated with dryness and overwork.

patterns.name: Blood stasis tendency
patterns.explanation: A notably dark tongue body is traditionally associated with 'blood stasis' — sluggish circulation.
